data.title: Bill Abbott names Paige Havins as Deputy General Counsel at Great American Media, adding her 10+ years of legal and transaction experience. She previously held the same role at a global tech protection company. Havins is also an adjunct law professor with a focus on mentorship and community service.
data.description: With over ten years of expertise in legal affairs and transactions, the individual has served as the former Deputy General Counsel at a leading global technology security firm. They also hold a role as an adjunct law professor, demonstrating a dedicated focus on mentorship and community involvement. The professional is based in New York and the information was released on January 12, 2026, by PRNewswire through Great American Media.
A bipartisan group of lawmakers, led by Senator Coons, is going to Greenland to meet with high-ranking Danish officials and business leaders, according to reporter on X.

data.title: World Liberty Financial, linked to Trump, launches $3.4 billion World Liberty Markets lending platform with $1 stablecoin. Critics raise conflict of interest concerns.
data.description: World Liberty Financial (CRYPTO: WLFI), supported by President Donald Trump's family, has introduced World Liberty Markets, a decentralized finance (DeFi) lending platform for its stablecoin valued at $1, amounting to $3.4 billion. However, critics have raised concerns over potential conflicts of interest relating to Trump's substantial $800 million cryptocurrency earnings in 2025. The platform enables users to engage in lending and borrowing operations using the USD1 stablecoin, which can be utilized as collateral alongside assets such as Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH), USDC, USDT, tokenized Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), and the WLFI governance token. Operated by the Dolomite protocol, users can currently either lend out or borrow USD1 at approximately 0.08% and 0.83% interest rates, respectively. It is anticipated that these rates will fluctuate as more capital flows into the system. Since its inception in March 2025, the USD1 stablecoin has witnessed a circulation surge to $3.4 billion, positioning it among the prominent dollar-backed stablecoins, trailing behind leading players like Tether and Circle (NYSE).

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch's stock dropped in heavy volume as company lowers full-year outlook to lower end of guidance.
data.description: A popular fashion retailer, Abercrombie & Fitch, experienced significant selling activity as its stock was traded heavily following a downward revision in its full-year forecast. The company adjusted its outlook to the lower range of its initial guidance, leading to a decline in its share price. This shift in performance comes amidst a broader trend of softening results among fashion retailers, marking a notable departure from their previous strong performance.

data.title: Alnylam stock slipped after Q4 preannouncement at J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. Day One's success highlighted.
data.description: Alnylam's stock experienced a decline on Monday following its fourth-quarter preannouncement before the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. It is reported that both Alnylam and Sarepta incurred losses while Day One emerged successful in the post JPM preannouncements.

data.title: SiteOne Landscape Supply stock's Relative Strength Rating upgrades as price performance improves.
data.description: SiteOne Landscape Supply has demonstrated an increase in its price performance, leading to an upgrade in its IBD Relative Strength Rating. This upgrade reflects the company's strong performance in comparison to other stocks. The stock's rating has seen a significant rise, indicating positive momentum in its price movement. This development showcases SiteOne Landscape Supply as a promising investment opportunity.

data.title: Ashley Companies enhance CSR through community partnerships and environmental efforts in 2025, expanding global impact.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida on January 12, 2026, the Ashley companies are furthering their dedication to environmental responsibility, community collaborations, and social endeavors globally. In the year 2025, Ashley enhanced initiatives focused on creating substantial impacts in these areas.

data.title: Susan G. Komen® is advocating to protect the breast cancer community from changes in health care that could increase costs and reduce coverage; focuses on 2026 advocacy priorities.
data.description: The Susan G. Komen® Center for Public Policy has unveiled its advocacy priorities for 2026, emphasizing the crucial task of safeguarding the breast cancer community against forthcoming alterations that threaten affordability and healthcare accessibility. Policymakers at state and federal levels are evaluating measures that may notably escalate expenses, diminish coverage, and undermine essential cancer initiatives. The proposed changes could jeopardize access to vital healthcare services for numerous individuals.

data.title: NetDocuments acquires eDOCS from OpenText, expanding to 90+ countries, focusing on legal professionals.
data.description: In Lehi, Utah, NetDocuments, a cloud-native legal document management platform with AI capabilities, has finalized its acquisition of eDOCS from OpenText. The agreement encompasses the entire product range and the specialized eDOCS team. This move broadens NetDocuments’ reach to more than 90 countries and ensures the eDOCS community has a lasting home within a company exclusively dedicated to serving legal professionals. The acquisition strengthens NetDocuments’ capacity to offer top-tier services on a global scale.

data.title: Despite $286M in Bitcoin sold by 'OG whales,' chances of $100K BTC are still high amidst distribution slowdown and bullish momentum.
data.description: In January, large Bitcoin holders, known as whales, collectively sold around $286 million worth of BTC. However, recent trends indicate a decrease in selling activity among these whales and a surge in positive market sentiment, potentially propelling Bitcoin's price to $100,000 by the end of the month.
